levels.description: The practice is ad-hoc and unstructured. Activities may be performed but without consistent procedures, documentation, or management oversight. Success depends on individual effort rather than organizational capability.
Basic procedures are established and followed for the practice. Activities are repeatable because knowledge exists within teams, though it may not be formally documented across the organization.
characteristics: Basic procedures exist and are followed
Resources are assigned to the practice

levels.description: The practice is formally defined, documented, and standardized across the organization. Roles and responsibilities are clear. Training is provided and the practice is consistently applied.
characteristics: Practice is formally documented and standardized
Roles and responsibilities are clearly defined
Process outputs are defined and measured
Interfaces with other practices are documented

levels.description: The practice is measured and managed using quantitative objectives. Performance is predictable and variations are understood. Data-driven decisions guide practice improvements.
characteristics: Quantitative objectives are defined for the practice
Performance metrics are collected and analyzed
Trends are monitored and acted upon

levels.description: The practice is continuously improved based on quantitative performance data and alignment with business objectives. Innovation is actively pursued and the practice is considered best-in-class.
characteristics: Best practices are shared and adopted
The practice is benchmarked against industry leaders
